name: 14k Pawn & Gun
description_short: 14K Pawn & Gun Westland buys and sells firearms, jewelry, tools, electronics, and collectibles for fast cash. Licensed FFL dealer offering same-day appraisals in Westland, MI.
description_long:
14K Pawn & Gun Westland is a locally-owned pawn shop operating in Westland, Michigan for nearly 12 years. The business positions itself as a community-focused alternative lender and reseller, operating under the tagline "You name it...We buy it! We pay more...Guaranteed!" They maintain a physical showroom and operate across multiple online sales channels including Gunbroker.com, eBay, and Facebook Marketplace to reach broader audiences.

The company's core business model centers on three revenue streams: purchasing used items from consumers for immediate cash, selling those items at retail markup, and offering collateral-based lending through pawning. Their inventory spans firearms and ammunition (their primary specialty), precious metals and jewelry, tools from major brands like Milwaukee and Snap-on, gaming systems (PS5, Xbox Series X, Nintendo Switch), computers (MacBooks, iPads), cameras, and general electronics. As a licensed Federal Firearms License (FFL) dealer, they are authorized to buy, sell, and transfer firearms and ammunition, which differentiates them from standard pawn shops.

Key service differentiators include rapid appraisal and cash disbursement (claiming under 10 minutes), a stated commitment to competitive pricing ("pay more"), knowledgeable staff assistance for firearms and ammunition selection, and the convenience of multiple transaction channels. Their firearms department operates slightly reduced hours (9am-5pm) compared to the main store (9am-6:15pm Monday-Saturday), reflecting regulatory compliance needs. The business actively manages inventory across physical and digital storefronts.

For consumers seeking immediate cash for used items, 14K offers a legitimate and transparent alternative to personal loans or credit-based solutions. However, customers should understand that pawn transactions are fundamentally different from loans—items are typically sold rather than held as collateral for repayment. The company's success depends on accurate appraisals and fair pricing; the "guaranteed" messaging around competitive pricing is their primary differentiation but cannot be independently verified.
pros: Licensed FFL firearms dealer with knowledgeable staff for gun and ammunition transactions
Fast cash appraisals with stated completion time under 10 minutes
Broad inventory categories (firearms, jewelry, tools, electronics, collectibles) reduces need for multiple vendors
Multi-channel selling (physical store, Gunbroker, eBay, Facebook) increases item sales velocity
Established local business (nearly 12 years operating) suggests community trust and operational stability
Extended store hours (9am-6:15pm weekdays and Saturday) accommodate working customers
Stated commitment to competitive pricing with 'pay more' guarantee
cons: Pawn transactions result in item sale, not collateral-based loans—customers lose ownership rather than maintaining repayment options
Appraisal values are subject to individual assessment with no stated appeal or valuation transparency process
Firearms department closes at 5pm daily, limiting access for after-work firearm transactions
No website pricing information, reviews, or valuation examples to set customer expectations
Closed Sundays, limiting weekend access for potential customers
best_for: Gun owners and enthusiasts needing to sell or trade firearms with FFL compliance assurance
Consumers with tools, jewelry, or electronics seeking immediate cash without loan obligations
Bargain hunters seeking discounted pre-owned firearms, tools, or collectibles
People needing quick liquidity who prefer asset sales over debt-based borrowing
diagnosis: 14K Pawn & Gun Westland is best for consumers with used items—especially firearms, tools, or electronics—who need immediate cash and prefer asset sales to credit-based borrowing. The primary caveat is that pawn shops sell items rather than lending against them; customers should understand they are liquidating assets, not securing a loan, and the appraised value depends on current market conditions and individual assessor judgment.
services: Federal Firearms License (FFL) dealer services and compliance
Buy and sell jewelry and coins
Buy and sell tools (Milwaukee, Snap-on, DeWalt, hand tools)
Buy and sell consumer electronics (gaming systems, MacBooks, iPads, cameras)
Online sales facilitation through Gunbroker.com, eBay, Facebook Marketplace
Text and phone communication for inquiries
